step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Multiple sources explicitly state that pulmonary toxicity is a well-known, dose-dependent adverse effect of bleomycin, with reports of up to 4% incidence and fatalities in some cases. Pathophysiological mechanisms describe direct lung injury through oxidative stress leading to inflammation and fibrosis, supported by observations of collagen accumulation and altered vascular function in lung tissues. Furthermore, guidelines recommend routine pulmonary monitoring (imaging, PFTs) and avoidance of supplemental oxygen shortly after treatment due to heightened risks. These findings collectively confirm that bleomycin causes lung toxicity.
